status: public synthetic card pack for reviewer attack
gmail_reply_state: No new substantive medical AI reply required action before build. Older T CAIREM route reply closed the direct meeting path and suggested public follow channels only.

cards.reviewer_check: Ask what evidence would be needed before public wording uses safety language.
cards.stop_condition: Block any claim that a score alone proves safety.

cards.reviewer_check: Ask whether the source supports the exact medical wording, population, care setting, date, and action boundary.
cards.stop_condition: Keep any unsupported source link in review.

cards.unsafe_pattern: Turkish wording sounds fluent but shifts risk
cards.reviewer_check: Ask what Turkish phrase changes the safety boundary.
cards.stop_condition: Block any term that can change clinical meaning across context until reviewed.

cards.reviewer_check: Ask which readiness gate is missing before hospital quality language is used.
cards.stop_condition: Block any hospital readiness claim without a documented readiness gate.

cards.unsafe_pattern: Synthetic card becomes real case evidence
cards.reviewer_check: Ask whether the card is being used as a pattern seed or as evidence of frequency.
cards.stop_condition: Block any frequency or outcome claim from a synthetic card.

cards.reviewer_check: Ask whether the wording gives an action instruction that should remain under clinician review.
cards.stop_condition: Block any patient instruction derived from broad policy wording.

cards.platform_lane: Health Data Quality and Label Audit Commons
cards.reviewer_check: Ask what data fitness evidence is missing.
cards.stop_condition: Block any data fitness claim without label and provenance review.

cards.reviewer_check: Ask what reviewer role, trigger, authority, and record are missing.
cards.stop_condition: Block any human review claim if role and authority are undefined.

cards.reviewer_check: Ask which vendor phrase is being converted into a medical assurance claim.
cards.stop_condition: Block any copied capability claim if it becomes a medical safety claim.

cards.reviewer_check: Ask whether the wording separates route exploration from clinical readiness.
cards.stop_condition: Block any deployment readiness wording unless the required formal process is complete and explicitly verified.

done_condition: Ten synthetic cards are public, validator passes, and the README plus roadmap point reviewers to the card pack.
